phrases.cultural_note: A winstub is a traditional Alsatian tavern. The name comes from 'Weinstube' (wine room). They serve local food and wine.

responses.english: It's a small traditional Alsatian restaurant.
responses.phonetic: say tun spay-see-ah-lee-TAY oo oon par-tee-kew-lah-ree-TAY loh-KAL
responses.french: C'est un petit restaurant alsacien traditionnel.

phrases.text_local: Je voudrais essayer la choucroute, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DREH ay-say-YAY lah shoo-KROOT, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Choucroute is sauerkraut cooked with wine and sausage. It's the signature dish of Alsace — hearty and delicious.

responses.english: Great choice. I'll bring it to you.
responses.phonetic: wee byan soor, see say dee-spoh-nee-BL
responses.french: Très bon choix. Je vous l'apporte.

phrases.cultural_note: Flammekueche (tarte flambée) is like a thin pizza with cream, onions, and bacon. A local specialty.

responses.phonetic: wee byan soor, see say dee-spoh-nee-BL
responses.french: Tout de suite. Avec de la crème fraîche.

phrases.text_local: Un vin d'Alsace, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: uhn van dal-SAS, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Try a Riesling or Gewürztraminer. Alsace is one of France's great wine regions, known for white wines.

responses.english: Try a Riesling or a Gewurztraminer.
responses.phonetic: byan sewr, zhuh voo ah-PROHNT sah too deh SWEET
responses.french: Essayez un Riesling ou un Gewurztraminer.

phrases.cultural_note: The bretzel originated in Alsace. The classic shape has a symbolic meaning — arms folded in prayer.

responses.english: Here you go, a bretzel. Enjoy.
responses.phonetic: wee byan soor, see say dee-spoh-nee-BL
